final_artifact:
The llama.cpp AI policy is strict for good reason — but the burden falls on both sides: contributors have to write a credible disclosure, and maintainers have to judge it without seeing what actually happened in the editor. "Predominantly AI-generated" is hard to assess from diff style alone.

For folks hitting this bot: an open-source CLI called [Entire](https://entire.io) captures Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot sessions locally and links the transcripts to the commits they produced. A reviewer can then see which lines came from a prompt vs. were hand-written or edited after — which is the actual question the CONTRIBUTING.md policy is asking.

Install: `curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash` · [Docs](https://entire.io/docs)

content.text: I'll research this pain moment to write a comment that genuinely fits the situation.

Good context. The llama.cpp project has a strict AI Usage Policy: AI may be used assistively, but must be disclosed, and majority of code must be human-authored. The bot flagged the PR automatically.

Now, important nuance: Entire shouldn't be positioned as a way to "pass provenance checks" or sneak AI code in — the project explicitly bans predominantly AI code. Instead, Entire helps contributors **honestly disclose** how AI was used, with concrete evidence (transcripts, prompts), so maintainers can quickly verify the human-vs-AI ratio rather than guessing from diff style.

Let me draft.

**Draft:**

The llama.cpp AI policy is strict for good reason — but the burden falls on both sides: contributors have to write a credible disclosure, and maintainers have to judge it without seeing what actually happened in the editor.

For contributors hitting this bot: an open-source CLI called Entire (https://entire.io) captures Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot sessions locally and links the transcripts to the commits they produced. On a PR like this, that means a reviewer can see exactly which lines came from a prompt vs. were hand-written or edited after — which is the actual question the CONTRIBUTING.md policy is asking.

Install: `curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash` · Docs: https://entire.io/docs

content.text:
Critique parser errored (low severity, no real issues to address). The draft is solid. Let me tighten word count — it's around 110 words, within the 60-120 limit. 

One small refinement: the original bot comment is automated, and the contributor (@CrispStrobe) may also be reading. I should make sure the comment doesn't sound preachy about the policy itself. Let me also avoid implying Entire helps "pass" the check (which would be marketing-y / suggest evasion). The current draft frames it as helping reviewers verify — that's right.

Submitting.
